title: Colony-stimulating factor-2 triggers NNMT-dependent myofibroblast activation in head and neck cancer
task: Characterize CSF2-driven myofibroblast activation independent of TGF-β
key_results: CSF2 from tumor cells induces myCAF properties in a NNMT-dependent manner; CSF2 phosphorylates FOS-Ser32, promoting p-FOS nuclear translocation and NNMT transcription; CSF2 blockade partially overcomes TGF-β inhibitor resistance
comments: Identifies non-canonical myCAF activation pathway; therapeutic targeting of CSF2 may selectively sensitize TGF-β-refractory tumors.

model_io.model: Head and neck cancer organoids, fibroblasts, and xenografts

hypotheses.hypothesis: CSF2 induces myoCAF activation independent of TGF-β signaling
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; NNMT induction by CSF2 unaffected by TGF-β presence

hypotheses.hypothesis: CSF2 activates NNMT transcription via FOS Ser32 phosphorylation and nuclear translocation
hypotheses.result: Supported by mechanistic data; CSF2-mediated FOS phosphorylation demonstrated

title: CAPRIN1-mediated sequestration of NCOA4 mRNA into stress granules drives sorafenib resistance in hepatocellular carcinoma
task: Define stress granule-mediated sorafenib resistance in hepatocellular carcinoma
key_results: SG signature stratifies HCC patients; high SG score correlates with poor survival and sorafenib resistance; CAPRIN1 sequesters NCOA4 mRNA in SGs, suppressing ferroptosis; CAPRIN1 disruption restores sorafenib sensitivity
comments: Identifies actionable SG-ferroptosis axis; RGG domain-NCOA4 mRNA interaction may be targetable.

model_io.model: HCC cell lines and patient samples

hypotheses.hypothesis: CAPRIN1 RGG domain mediates NCOA4 mRNA sequestration in SGs
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; CAPRIN1-NCOA4 mRNA interaction leads to NCOA4 translational repression

hypotheses.hypothesis: NCOA4 repression suppresses sorafenib-induced ferroptosis
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; CAPRIN1 disruption restores NCOA4 and resensitizes resistant tumors

title: ZNRF1 deficiency disrupts Fas ligand trafficking and immune balance
task: Characterize ZNRF1's role in FasL trafficking and immune regulation
key_results: Myeloid ZNRF1 deficiency causes age-dependent splenomegaly and elevated CD4+/CD8+ ratios; ZNRF1 E3 activity required for Munc18-2–Syntaxin-3 interaction and FasL surface docking; impaired FasL-dependent killing of activated T cells
comments: Elegantly links E3 ligase catalytic activity to SNARE complex function; mechanism of ZNRF1-mediated ubiquitination target(s) remains unidentified.

model_io.model: Myeloid-specific Znrf1-deficient mice and macrophages

hypotheses.hypothesis: ZNRF1 E3 activity is required for optimal Munc18-2–Stx3 interaction
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; ZNRF1 C184A catalytic mutant restored FasL surface levels despite similar complex assembly

hypotheses.hypothesis: ZNRF1 deficiency impairs terminal FasL trafficking and docking
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; late docking/fusion defect observed despite preserved lysosome-related organelle polarization

title: NUP93 facilitates the nuclear import of SOX2 to activate G3BP1 transcription and impairs gemcitabine response in pancreatic cancer
task: Determine NUP93's role in gemcitabine resistance in PDAC
key_results: NUP93 upregulation correlates with poor survival; NUP93/SOX2/G3BP1 axis promotes DNA repair and gemcitabine resistance; axis disruption synergizes with gemcitabine in vivo
comments: Elegant mechanistic pathway linking nuclear transport to chemoresistance; therapeutic targeting of protein-protein interactions may face bioavailability challenges.

model_io.model: PDAC cell lines and xenografts

hypotheses.hypothesis: NUP93 facilitates SOX2 nuclear import via NLS recognition
hypotheses.result: Confirmed by NUP93-SOX2 interaction and enhanced nuclear SOX2 localization

hypotheses.hypothesis: Nuclear SOX2 activates G3BP1 transcription
hypotheses.result: Confirmed by SOX2 binding to G3BP1 promoter and increased G3BP1 expression

title: LMO7-mediated POLR2A degradation promotes cellular senescence through the MDM4/p53/p21 axis
task: Define POLR2A's protective role against cellular senescence
key_results: POLR2A decline observed in senescent cells; POLR2A depletion induces senescence via p53/p21 pathway; LMO7 E3 ligase promotes POLR2A ubiquitination and degradation
comments: Identifies LMO7 as a previously uncharacterized POLR2A regulator; therapeutic benefit of POLR2A stabilization in aging-related diseases remains speculative.

model_io.model: Cultured cells and aging mouse tissues

hypotheses.hypothesis: POLR2A depletion induces senescence through p53-dependent pathway
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; p53 co-depletion rescues senescence phenotype

hypotheses.hypothesis: LMO7 mediates POLR2A degradation during senescence
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; LMO7 depletion abolishes POLR2A ubiquitination in H₂O₂-induced senescence

title: A bridge-like lipid transfer protein is critical for generation of invasive stages in malaria parasites
task: Identify lipid transfer mechanisms required for malaria parasite schizogony
key_results: PfVPS13L1 bridges ER-inner membrane complex (IMC) contact sites; PfVPS13L1 loss reduces IMC growth and generates smaller anucleated progeny, impairing schizogony
comments: First characterization of lipid transfer at ER-MCS in apicomplexans; functional significance for parasite biology well-established.

model_io.model: P. falciparum blood stage parasites ± PfVPS13L1

hypotheses.hypothesis: PfVPS13L1 mediates bulk lipid transfer from ER to nascent IMC
hypotheses.result: Supported by loss-of-function data showing reduced IMC growth and smaller progeny

title: 7SK-enriched exosomes suppress the cancer phenotypes in human lung cancer cells: insights from 2D and microfluidic 3D in vitro models
task: Evaluate exosome-delivered 7SK lncRNA efficacy against lung cancer
key_results: Exo-7SK reduced cell viability and proliferation, promoted apoptosis, inhibited migration/invasion in 2D and impaired spheroid formation in 3D microfluidic cultures
comments: Demonstrates functional advantages of 3D culture models; clinical translation requires optimization of exosome manufacturing and biodistribution.

model_io.model: Non-small-cell lung cancer (2D and 3D microfluidic)

hypotheses.hypothesis: 7SK lncRNA suppresses cancer phenotypes via gene expression modulation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; Exo-7SK shifted expression away from EMT and reduced cancer traits

title: Biological effects of 5G-modulated 700 MHz RF-EMF exposure on neuronal and glial cell models under isothermal conditions
task: Assess biological effects of 700 MHz 5G-modulated RF-EMF on neural cells
key_results: No statistically significant differences in mitochondrial ROS, cell viability, apoptosis, or astrocyte proliferation at SAR 0.08–4 W/kg under isothermal conditions; positive controls validated assay sensitivity
comments: Well-controlled in vitro study under isothermal conditions; findings do not exclude non-thermal effects or in vivo relevance.

model_io.model: Primary rat cortical astrocytes and SH-SY5Y neuroblastoma cells exposed in TEM cells

hypotheses.hypothesis: 5G-modulated 700 MHz RF-EMF induces oxidative stress and apoptosis at isothermal SAR levels
hypotheses.result: Rejected; no measurable effects detected across all exposure conditions

title: GABARAPL1 is important for the activation of HRI during eIF2α phosphorylation-dependent stress response to sodium arsenite
task: Elucidate GABARAPL1's role in HRI activation during arsenite stress
key_results: GABARAPL1 deficiency reduced eIF2α phosphorylation and impaired stress granule formation; GABARAPL1 facilitates HSP90-HRI interaction for full HRI kinase activation
comments: Interesting cross-talk between autophagy machinery and integrated stress response; mechanism of HSP90-HRI facilitation warrants structural validation.

model_io.model: A549 lung cancer cells ± GABARAPL1

hypotheses.hypothesis: GABARAPL1 facilitates HSP90-HRI interaction for full HRI kinase activation
hypotheses.result: Supported by reduced eIF2α phosphorylation and stress granule defects in GABARAPL1-absent cells

title: Small-molecule binding and sensing with a designed protein family
task: Design de novo small-molecule-binding proteins with designable pocket geometries
key_results: Achieved nanomolar to low micromolar binding affinities with atomic-level design accuracy; cortisol biosensor successfully constructed
comments: Combines deep learning and physics-based design effectively; NTF2 fold platform shows potential but scalability to diverse targets needs assessment.

model_io.model: Deep learning + physics-based design pipeline
outputs: designed protein binders with characterized binding affinities

model_io.model: Chemically induced dimerization (CID) system

hypotheses.hypothesis: NTF2 fold family can achieve diverse designable pocket geometries
hypotheses.result: Successfully designed binders for six small-molecule targets with nanomolar affinity

title: Neoantigen evolution and response to checkpoint inhibitor immunotherapy in colorectal cancer
task: Relate neoantigen evolution and tumor mutational heterogeneity to checkpoint-inhibitor immunotherapy response in colorectal cancer
key_results: Strong clonal neoantigen crucial for successful immunotherapy response; minimal neoantigen quality across all contributing neoantigens is strongest predictor of durable response
comments: Integrates stochastic tumor evolution with dynamical immune dynamics; provides mechanistic insight into response heterogeneity but requires prospective clinical validation.

model_io.model: Stochastic branching-process model with immunogenicity prediction
outputs: in-silico tumor neoantigen landscapes labeled by predicted immunogenicity

model_io.model: Dynamical systems model of tumor-immune interactions

hypotheses.hypothesis: Strong clonal neoantigens are crucial for durable checkpoint-inhibitor response
hypotheses.result: Strong clonal neoantigen identified as critical for successful response; minimal neoantigen quality strongest predictor of durability

title: A cough sound-based deep learning algorithm for accessible prompt detection of chronic obstructive pulmonary disease with smartphones
task: Detect COPD from cough sounds using transformer-based deep learning on smartphones
key_results: External validation AUC 0.94; sensitivity 92%, specificity 86%; >93% sensitivity for severe stages (GOLD 3–4); robust across non-COPD respiratory conditions and smartphone models
comments: Impressive real-world performance enables accessible COPD screening, but requires clinical integration studies and long-term diagnostic utility assessment.

model_io.model: Transformer-based neural network (Cough Search)
inputs: voluntary cough sound recordings from smartphones

hypotheses.hypothesis: Transformer networks extract diagnostic features from cough sounds for COPD detection
hypotheses.result: Achieved AUC 0.94 and 92% sensitivity/86% specificity in external validation

title: The SjD Map: an interactive pathway tour into Sjögren’s disease signalling mechanisms
task: Map Sjögren's disease signalling mechanisms via comprehensive molecular interaction network
key_results: Identified 1,625 DEGs; 25 shared across three datasets; 9 common DEGs linked to interferon signalling; 43 pathways integrated into MIM with 829 molecular entities and 598 interactions
comments: Comprehensive resource integrates multi-dataset transcriptomics with curated pathways; biological validation and therapeutic target prioritization remain needed.

model_io.model: Differential expression analysis
inputs: peripheral blood samples from SjD patients vs. healthy controls (GSE51092, UKPSSR, PRECISESADS)

model_io.model: Pathway enrichment and molecular interaction mapping
outputs: SjD Map with 829 entities and 598 interactions

hypotheses.hypothesis: Interferon signalling is central to SjD pathogenesis
hypotheses.result: 9 common DEGs linked to interferon signalling across all datasets

title: KinForm: kinetics-informed feature optimised representation models for enzyme kcat and KM prediction
task: Predict enzyme kinetic parameters (kcat, KM) using optimized protein representations
key_results: Outperformed baselines on two benchmarks; largest improvements in low-sequence-similarity bins; 99.46% dimensionality reduction while preserving discriminative information
comments: Intelligent feature engineering (binding-site pooling, intermediate-layer selection, PCA, oversampling) addresses data scarcity; similarity-based evaluation provides realistic generalization estimates.

model_io.model: KinForm (machine learning framework)
inputs: residue-level embeddings from multiple ESM and ProtT5 models

model_io.model: Principal component analysis and similarity-based oversampling
inputs: training data with protein similarity metrics

hypotheses.hypothesis: Binding-site-informed pooling and intermediate-layer selection improve kinetic prediction
hypotheses.result: Binding-site probability pooling, intermediate-layer selection, PCA, and oversampling each contributed to performance gains

title: From short-lived to persistent: the significance of extracellular RNA in disinfected drinking water microbiomes
task: Characterize microbial adaptive mechanisms in disinfected drinking water using extracellular RNA as reference
key_results: eRNA reference identified energy efficiency, dynamic membrane composition, oxidative stress response, and protein folding as critical survival features; antibiotic resistance genes upregulated in intracellular fraction; ncRNA and tmRNA found in eRNA suggest regulatory roles
comments: Novel eRNA-based framework addresses lack of baseline in environmental settings but requires validation of mechanistic inferences through functional studies.

model_io.model: RNA-based differential transcriptomics
inputs: intracellular RNA from drinking water microbiomes

hypotheses.hypothesis: eRNA provides representative baseline for identifying adaptive features in drinking water microbiomes
hypotheses.result: Energy efficiency, membrane composition, oxidative stress response, and protein folding identified as critical; antibiotic resistance genes upregulated

title: An optimized EfficientNetB0 framework with CLAHE-based preprocessing for accurate multi-class chest X-ray classification
task: Multi-label chest X-ray classification with contrast enhancement and class balancing
key_results: Macro-average AUC 0.906, recall 0.824; Pneumonia AUC 0.950, Cardiomegaly AUC 0.946; outperformed DenseNet121 and MobileNetV2
comments: CLAHE preprocessing and strategic class balancing effectively address multi-label complexity, but clinical deployment requires prospective validation.

model_io.model: EfficientNetB0 with transfer learning

hypotheses.hypothesis: CLAHE-based preprocessing improves multi-label classification of overlapping pathologies
hypotheses.result: Framework achieved superior diagnostic performance (AUC 0.906) with strong per-class discrimination

title: Machine learning-predicted chromatin organization landscape across pediatric tumors
task: Predict structural variant-induced chromatin disruptions in pediatric tumors
key_results: Identified five recurrently disrupted regions across multiple tumor types; SVs from lymphomas/sarcomas most disruptive; method highlighted novel candidate loci near oncogenes
comments: ML-based prioritization of SVs shows promise but requires experimental validation of functional relevance in tumorigenesis.

model_io.model: Akita (convolutional neural network)
inputs: somatic structural variants from 61 tumor types

model_io.model: Modified Activity-by-Contact scoring

hypotheses.hypothesis: High-impact SVs cluster in specific genomic regions across tumors
hypotheses.result: Five recurrently disrupted regions identified enriched for high-impact SVs

title: The impact of PD-1 mutations on pembrolizumab binding: insights from molecular dynamics and MM-GBSA analysis
task: Assess impact of PD-1 mutations on pembrolizumab binding affinity
key_results: 23 of 28 mutations reduced binding affinity; Pro89Arg decreased binding free energy by 26.69 kcal/mol; Asp85Gly and Asp85Asn abolished critical salt bridges
comments: Comprehensive MD/MM-GBSA analysis identifies plausible resistance mutations, but experimental validation essential before clinical interpretation.

model_io.model: Molecular dynamics simulations (50 ns)
outputs: hydrogen bonding and salt bridge stability

model_io.model: MM-GBSA free energy calculations

hypotheses.hypothesis: Specific PD-1 mutations reduce pembrolizumab binding affinity
hypotheses.result: 23 mutations reduced affinity; Pro89Arg, Asp85Gly, Asp85Asn showed largest reductions (18–27 kcal/mol)

title: Few-shot android malware classification with quantum-enhanced prototypical learning and drift detection
task: Few-shot Android malware classification with concept drift detection and explainability
key_results: 99.70% accuracy (CCCS-CIC-AndMal-2020, 15 families); 99.33% (KronoDroid); 99.46% dimensionality reduction; 0.24% max accuracy degradation over time; file descriptor manipulation and file system operations most discriminative
comments: Few-shot prototypical networks with CatBoost feature selection achieve state-of-the-art performance; quantum enhancement and drift detection add robustness but need empirical justification.

model_io.model: CatBoost feature selection
inputs: high-dimensional malware feature sets (9,503 and 489 features)

model_io.model: Prototypical network with episodic meta-learning
Concept drift detection and XAI (SHAP, LIME)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Few-shot prototypical learning with intelligent feature selection enables accurate malware classification with minimal annotation
hypotheses.result: 99.70% accuracy with 5 support samples per class; robust temporal stability (0.24% max degradation)

title: Characterisation of thigh-based electrocardiography (ECG) across different pathologies
task: Assess feasibility of thigh-based ECG monitoring via toilet-seat interface for cardiovascular disease classification
key_results: Clear P-QRS-T complexes preserved in thigh-acquired ECGs; disease-specific HRV patterns detected (SDNN to 140 ms, RMSSD/pNN50 reductions in valve disease); aortic stenosis most distant from normal cluster
comments: Novel unobtrusive monitoring approach shows promise for home-based screening but requires validation in larger cohorts and real-world adherence studies.

model_io.model: ECG signal processing and HRV analysis
inputs: thigh-based ECG signals from toilet-seat electrodes

model_io.model: Principal component analysis and hierarchical clustering
inputs: multi-feature ECG data from 30 patients with diverse cardiovascular conditions

hypotheses.hypothesis: Thigh-based ECGs preserve morphology and HRV information for disease stratification
hypotheses.result: Clear P-QRS-T complexes obtained; disease-specific HRV patterns identified; aortic stenosis most distant from normal

title: Numerical study on the performance of a forced ventilation system under hydrogen leakage in an underground hydrogen equipment room
task: Hydrogen leakage ventilation system performance under transient conditions
key_results: Simple model effectively predicts volume-averaged H2 concentration and time to reach 1 and 4 vol% thresholds
comments: RANS CFD validation on simple model is pragmatic; parametric study helps design effective ventilation protocols.

model_io.model: 3D transient RANS (ANSYS Fluent) + simplified volume-averaged model

hypotheses.hypothesis: Simple model predicts volume-averaged H2 concentration trend and critical threshold times
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; model agrees with CFD results

title: Enhancing MTC device security in LTE networks for the fast and secure BC-based group handover authentication protocol
task: Blockchain-based group handover authentication for MTC devices in LTE with relay optimization
key_results: Significant reduction in energy consumption, authentication delay, and distance; improved network lifetime via Chimp Optimization Algorithm relay selection
comments: Combines ChOA for relay selection with blockchain for decentralized authentication; simulation validates effectiveness but lacks quantitative benchmark comparisons.

model_io.model: Chimp Optimization Algorithm (ChOA) + blockchain-based authentication protocol

hypotheses.hypothesis: ChOA-optimized relay selection with blockchain authentication improves energy efficiency and reduces delay
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via simulation

title: An embedded deep learning framework for real-time violence detection and alert generation
task: Real-time embedded violence detection using CNN-GRU with optimization for edge devices
key_results: Average accuracy 96.9%, F1 0.963, ROC-AUC 0.972 across 5 benchmark datasets; 26 FPS and 38.4 ms/frame on Raspberry Pi 5; outperforms I3D, LRCN, ViT
comments: System-level contribution: quantization, pruning, TensorRT acceleration for embedded deployment; Grad-CAM interpretability validates localization; statistical significance confirmed (p<0.05).

model_io.model: Lightweight CNN backbone + GRU temporal modeling + quantization/pruning/TensorRT
inputs: Video frames (Hockey Fight, RLVS, Violent Flows, ShanghaiTech, UCF-Crime, custom 6-class)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Optimized CNN-GRU with TensorRT achieves real-time throughput on resource-constrained devices while maintaining accuracy
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 26 FPS, 38.4 ms/frame on Raspberry Pi 5

title: Mathematical modeling of adaptive information security strategies using composite behavior models
task: Adaptive cybersecurity modeling using composite behavior and game theory
key_results: Strong improvements in threat detection, mitigation speed, and resource optimization; superior scalability and effectiveness vs. existing methods
comments: Claims superiority based on statistical hypothesis testing and simulation but lacks concrete quantitative benchmarks for comparison.

model_io.model: Composite behavior model + system dynamics + game theory framework

hypotheses.hypothesis: Unified dynamic model combining composite behaviors outperforms isolated models
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via simulation and statistical testing

title: Exploring the importance of shape on dynamic recognition of self-face or friend-face
task: Face recognition emphasizing shape vs. motion for self vs. friend identification
key_results: Self-face motion identification heavily dependent on face shape; friend-face motion recognition independent of observed shape
comments: Behavioral study using Deepfake manipulation; findings suggest distinct perceptual mechanisms for self-face recognition but limited to two conditions.

model_io.model: Deepfake-based dynamic face video generation with shape/motion manipulation

hypotheses.hypothesis: Self-face motion identification depends on face shape more than friend-face identification
hypotheses.result: Confirmed across both experiments

title: Confidence-calibrated federated graph attention for internet of things agents under latency SLOs
task: Federated graph attention for calibrated IoT agent network enforcement under latency SLOs
key_results: ROC-AUC/PR-AUC ≈0.97–0.98 with improved calibration; enforcement p99 latency 27.5 ms vs. 39.2 ms for baseline; compliance with <100 ms SLO
comments: Parameter-efficient federated learning (≤1.1 MB/round) with hardware ECDF validation on real edge devices (Raspberry Pi 5, Jetson Orin Nano) is rigorous; energy/carbon accounting adds practical depth.

model_io.model: HP-FedGAT-Trust-IBN (federated graph attention + LoRA/PEFT + trust-weighted aggregation + intent verification)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Federated graph attention with LoRA updates achieves SLO compliance while maintaining high calibration
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; p99 enforcement latency 27.5 ms satisfies <100 ms SLO

title: IoT enabled offshore infrastructure management and its impact on monitoring maintenance safety compliance and structural resilience
task: IoT implementation impact on offshore infrastructure management
key_results: IoT significantly improves monitoring visibility, data-driven maintenance, and safety compliance; resilience linked to early fault detection
comments: Questionnaire-based survey study lacks quantitative performance metrics; findings are empirically modeled but not experimentally validated.

model_io.model: Structural equation modeling / path analysis
outputs: Relationships between IoT implementation and operational outcomes

hypotheses.hypothesis: IoT implementation improves real-time monitoring and predictive maintenance
hypotheses.result: Confirmed through survey analysis

hypotheses.hypothesis: Resilience improvements are driven by early fault detection rather than direct structural diagnostics
hypotheses.result: Confirmed

title: Rigorous construction and classification of solitary-waves and exact soliton configurations in the nonlinear coupled Maccari system
task: Exact soliton and traveling-wave solutions of the nonlinear coupled Maccabi system
key_results: GERF method yields multiple solution classes: non-topological and topological solitons, exponential, kink-type, and periodic singular waves
comments: Analytical framework via wave-variable reduction to ODEs; contributions are mathematical rather than numerical or experimental.

model_io.model: Generalized Exponential Rational Function (GERF) method + wave-variable transformation

hypotheses.hypothesis: GERF method systematically constructs exact solutions of nonlinear evolution equations
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; multiple solution classes derived

title: A three-level discretization framework for dynamic behaviors of graphene-coated rotational blades with preset-angle sandwich structure under complex loads
task: Dynamic response of graphene-coated rotational turbine blades under pulse loads
key_results: Chebyshev-Ritz-Galerkin discretization overcomes FEM convergence challenges; systematic parametric study reveals GPL fraction and rotational speed synergy
comments: Three-level discretization is novel for high-speed rotation; comprehensive but primarily theoretical without experimental validation.

model_io.model: Three-level Chebyshev-Ritz-Galerkin + Halpin-Tsai micromechanical model
outputs: Vibration suppression, impact resistance, fatigue performance

hypotheses.hypothesis: Synergistic effect of GPL weight fraction and rotational speed improves dynamic performance
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via parametric study

title: Topology optimization of wheel spoke cavities for lightweight design under bending fatigue and impact load cases
task: Topology optimization of aluminum wheel cavities for lightweight design
key_results: Peak von Mises stress reduced 19.25% for bending fatigue (133.9→108.13 MPa), 14.57% for impact (74.63→63.76 MPa); mass reduction 0.53%
comments: Multi-load-case compromise programming with manufacturing constraints (10 mm member size, casting-compatible); modest mass reduction suggests conservative approach.

model_io.model: Region-constrained topology optimization with compromise programming
inputs: Manufacturing constraints (min member size, draw direction)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Multi-load-case topology optimization with strain energy weighting reduces peak stress while respecting manufacturing constraints
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 19.25% fatigue stress reduction achieved

title: A structure-preserving diffusion-based zero-shot learning framework for multimodal magnetic flux leakage signal analysis
task: Zero-shot learning for magnetic flux leakage pipeline defect detection
key_results: Zero-Shot Learning Accuracy 0.84, H-Mean 0.88 on unseen defects; macro F1 0.93 on known classes; SNR improvement 12.3→24.1 dB
comments: Multi-stage pipeline (SPDM denoising → cross-modal fusion → zero-shot recognizer) is comprehensive but complex; strong generalization shown on cross-material/pipeline tests.

model_io.model: Structure-Preserving Diffusion Model (SPDM) + gated cross-modal attention + visual-semantic zero-shot recognizer
inputs: Semantic attributes (geometry, depth, causal type, directionality)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Triple-constraint diffusion preserves defect edges better than standard denoising
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; SNR improvement from 12.3 to 24.1 dB

hypotheses.hypothesis: Zero-shot learning via semantic embedding enables recognition of unseen defect classes
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; ZSL Accuracy 0.84, H-Mean 0.88

title: Predictive patterning via solid-state dewetting of transferred single-crystal films
task: Predicting and controlling single-crystal film dewetting patterns via templating
key_results: Anisotropic Pd(100) dewetting guided by <001>/<011> facet preference; demonstrated submicron electrode channel fabrication for thin-film transistors
comments: Multiscale computational-experimental approach enables rational design; crystallographic alignment and ambient control are critical tuning parameters.

model_io.model: Multiscale dewetting simulation of single-crystal Pd films

hypotheses.hypothesis: Surface energy and gas adsorption anisotropies drive preferential facet formation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; <011> facets favored at high oxygen adsorption or hydrogen flow rate

title: The role of the Helmholtz potential on electrocatalytic activity
task: Predicting electrocatalytic activity from Helmholtz potential
key_results: Physical limit for hydrogen evolution reaction exchange current density is 10 A cm⁻²; thin semiconductor overlayers (1–10 nm) enhance activity via Helmholtz potential modulation
comments: Integrating electronic equilibrium into Butler-Volmer kinetics provides mechanistic insight, though experimental validation beyond literature data would strengthen predictive power.

model_io.model: Butler-Volmer kinetics with Helmholtz potential

hypotheses.hypothesis: Electrochemical kinetics governed by both Sabatier principle and Helmholtz potential
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via literature data on hydrogen evolution reaction

hypotheses.hypothesis: Thin semiconductor overlayers enhance activity through Helmholtz potential effects
hypotheses.result: Demonstrated as mechanism for activity enhancement

title: Prediction of ambient-pressure high-temperature superconductivity in electronically modified transition-metal hydrides
task: Predicting high-Tc superconductivity in carrier-doped transition-metal hydrides
key_results: MgAlFeH₆ predicted Tc ≈ 130 K; average projected DOS (geometric mean of total and H-projected DOS) correlates more strongly with Tc than individual DOS measures
comments: Expanding superconductor search to non-metallic hydrides is conceptually valuable; the average projected DOS metric is computationally efficient, though dynamic stability-Tc tradeoff limits practical applicability.

model_io.model: First-principles electronic structure calculations
outputs: density of states at Fermi level

hypotheses.hypothesis: Average projected DOS predicts Tc better than total or H-projected DOS alone
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; stronger correlation observed for transition-metal hydrides

hypotheses.hypothesis: Carrier doping enables high-Tc superconductivity in intrinsically non-metallic hydrides
hypotheses.result: Demonstrated for MgAlFeH₆ with predicted Tc ≈ 130 K

title: Electron-phonon physics at the exascale: a hybrid MPI-GPU-OpenMP framework for scalable Wannier interpolation
task: GPU acceleration of Wannier interpolation for electron-phonon calculations at exascale
key_results: 29-fold speedup on leadership supercomputers (Vista, Perlmutter, Aurora); enables calculations on 20 nm stanene nanoribbons previously intractable
comments: Hybrid MPI-GPU-OpenMP framework achieves near-ideal scalability to thousands of GPU nodes; seamless portability across NVIDIA, AMD, Intel accelerators is significant.

model_io.model: EPW code with GPU-accelerated Wannier interpolation

hypotheses.hypothesis: GPU porting of Wannier interpolation maintains EPW algorithmic structure while achieving massive speedup
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 29-fold speedup with nearly ideal scaling to exascale

title: A low-complexity compact dual-polarized patch antenna with high isolation and low cross-polarization for in-band full-duplex applications
task: Designing compact dual-polarized patch antenna for full-duplex applications
key_results: Antenna size 0.18λ × 0.18λ × 0.03λ at 5.6 GHz; port isolation >38 dB, broadside gain 4.3 dBi, cross-polarization <−40 dB
comments: Capacitive loading technique elegantly achieves both size reduction and cross-polarization suppression; outperforms related designs in isolation and compactness.

model_io.model: Single-layer microstrip patch antenna with capacitive loading

hypotheses.hypothesis: Capacitive loading suppresses cross-polarization while reducing size
hypotheses.result: Confirmed experimentally; achieved <−40 dB cross-polarization and 0.18λ × 0.18λ footprint

title: Finite integration time can shift optimal sensitivity away from criticality
task: Optimizing neural network sensitivity under finite integration time constraints
key_results: Optimal dynamic regime shifts away from criticality for finite integration times; sensitivity depends on available integration period rather than theoretical maximum
comments: This work reconciles the theory-practice gap in criticality-enhanced sensing; finite-time effects likely dominate real biological and engineered systems.

model_io.model: Recurrent neural network near phase transition

hypotheses.hypothesis: Optimal sensitivity is maximized at criticality for infinite integration time
hypotheses.result: Confirmed theoretically; shifts away from criticality for finite time windows
